req.title: Close encounter
req.statement: A clinician shall close an Encounter; closed Encounters shall reject further modifications except for addendum Observations.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct test axes: (1) the happy-path state transition open→finished (STC/EP), (2) rejection of modifications to a closed encounter (NEG/DT), and (3) the boundary moment immediately after closing where the only permitted write is an addendum Observation (BVA). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and, where needed, pre-close a fresh encounter, ensuring idempotency. Error paths assert HTTP 409 per the spec. The addendum boundary test asserts that a POST of an Observation with derivedFrom pointing to the closed encounter is accepted (2xx), while a direct PATCH on the closed encounter itself is rejected. Test[4] (close nonexistent encounter) is a negative test covering an invalid input partition; its heuristic has been corrected from EP to NEG to satisfy the rule that negative-kind tests must not use EP.

tests.name: test_close_open_encounter_sets_status_finished
actions: POST /Encounter/{id}/close with valid clinician auth token
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_close_open_encounter_sets_status_finished(api_base_url, auth_headers, open_encounter):
    enc_id = open_encounter["id"]

    close_resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/Encounter/{enc_id}/close",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert close_resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {close_resp.status_code}"

    body = close_resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "finished", f"Body status: {body.get('status')}"

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/Encounter/{enc_id}",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "finished"

tests.name: test_patch_closed_encounter_returns_409
preconditions: An Encounter exists with status=finished (already closed)
actions: PATCH /Encounter/{id} with a payload that attempts to update a non-addendum field (e.g. subject)
expected: Response body contains an error indicating the encounter is closed
trace: R-HLT-007: error pathway – 409 when modifying closed encounter
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_patch_closed_encounter_returns_409(api_base_url, auth_headers, closed_encounter):
    enc_id = closed_encounter["id"]

    patch_payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "id": enc_id,
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/different-patient-99"},
    }

    resp = requests.patch(
        f"{api_base_url}/Encounter/{enc_id}",
        json=patch_payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409, f"Expected 409, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

    error_body = resp.json()
    assert "closed" in str(error_body).lower() or "finished" in str(error_body).lower()

tests.name: test_addendum_observation_allowed_immediately_after_close
actions: Immediately POST /Observation with category=addendum and encounter reference to the now-closed encounter
expected: Created Observation references the closed encounter
trace: R-HLT-007: closed Encounters shall reject further modifications EXCEPT for addendum Observations
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_addendum_observation_allowed_immediately_after_close(api_base_url, auth_headers, open_encounter):
    enc_id = open_encounter["id"]

    close_resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/Encounter/{enc_id}/close",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert close_resp.status_code == 200, f"Close failed: {close_resp.status_code}"

    obs_payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"code": "addendum"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "11506-3"}]},
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{enc_id}"},
        "valueString": "Addendum note post-close",
    }

    obs_resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/Observation",
        json=obs_payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert obs_resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 for addendum, got {obs_resp.status_code}: {obs_resp.text}"
    created = obs_resp.json()
    assert created["encounter"]["reference"] == f"Encounter/{enc_id}"

tests.name: test_close_already_closed_encounter_returns_conflict_or_idempotent
actions: POST /Encounter/{id}/close a second time on an already-finished encounter
expected: Server returns HTTP 409 Conflict OR HTTP 200 with status still finished (idempotent behaviour)
Encounter status remains finished in either case
trace: R-HLT-007: decision table row – close action applied when current state is already finished
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_close_already_closed_encounter_returns_conflict_or_idempotent(api_base_url, auth_headers, closed_encounter):
    enc_id = closed_encounter["id"]

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/Encounter/{enc_id}/close",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 409), (
        f"Expected 200 (idempotent) or 409 (conflict), got {resp.status_code}"
    )

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/Encounter/{enc_id}",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "finished", "Status must remain finished"

tests.name: test_close_nonexistent_encounter_returns_404
preconditions: Encounter ID does not exist in the system
expected: No encounter resource is created or modified
trace: R-HLT-007: precondition – encounter must exist and be open; supplying a non-existent ID is an invalid/negative input that must be rejected with 404
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

NONEXISTENT_ID = "nonexistent-encounter-00000"

def test_close_nonexistent_encounter_returns_404(api_base_url, auth_headers):
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/Encounter/{NONEXISTENT_ID}/close",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 404, f"Expected 404, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
